title: strychnos cuniculina
intro: Strychnos cuniculina is a species of plant that belongs to the Loganiaceae family, commonly known as the Strangle fig or Wild Strangle fig. This plant has been traditionally used in African and Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Strychnos cuniculina are attributed to its alkaloids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its various health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, Strychnos cuniculina is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although it's essential to note that its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
